behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser validation/formatting logic for a single expression. No OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output. Rejected Integration because behavior text is only about rejecting invalid syntax/value ranges (e.g., month 13, day 32, malformed syntax), which can be caught at one function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call parser directly with invalid cron strings and assert structured error type plus message content identifies the invalid field/token.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard and edge cron semantics.
behaviors.justification: This is calendar/cron computation logic ('last day of month', 'nearest weekday') in the calculator itself. No real infrastructure is required. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets correctness of returned timestamps from the calculator, not module handoff or persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke next-run calculator with fixed start instants and expressions including L/W-style edge cases; assert exact next N timestamps.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflict windows.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is interval/gap comparison and conflict-window reporting logic. It can be reproduced with deterministic schedule-fire inputs in-process. Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about overlap detection outcome/reporting, not cross-module reliability or external state.
behaviors.test_description: Feed detector two deterministic schedule streams (or schedule objects with fixed generated times) and a min-gap (e.g., 5 minutes); assert detected overlap windows are exact and complete.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for 2:30 AM jobs.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at module boundary between cron-time calculation and timezone/DST resolution rules. A single isolated function mock can miss handoff mistakes (local-time intent vs zoned instant mapping). No real OS/network/subprocess is required, so not System. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule calculator with real timezone rules in-process (IANA zone data from runtime library), then assert expected behavior for 2:30 AM across known spring-forward and fall-back transition dates in a target timezone.

behaviors.description: Engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill) when next run is already in the past.
behaviors.justification: Failure is policy decision after combining computed next-run state with job configuration; this is cross-module orchestration (scheduler state + policy evaluator). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real subprocess/OS/network; deterministic in-process time and state are sufficient. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Construct jobs with different missed-run policies and past-due computed next-run times in an in-process scheduler service; assert emitted decision/action is skip or backfill per policy and expected run window.
